0e1ea5bb-ff9a-4c64-a0b7-898ec2834088.title: Projects
0e1ea5bb-ff9a-4c64-a0b7-898ec2834088.content:
Voice order


company: Maxim Technology

Implemented from scratch to high-CCU production and became a maintainer of a backend microservice. The service enables taxi passengers order a taxi via giving commands as an answer to voice guidance. Supplied metrics from application and customized both JVM and business metrics in Grafana. By implementing both blocking and asynchronous Asterisk operations in combinations of Java asynchronous features the service supports asynchronous communication - a person can interrupt automated responses and, if necessary, be transferred to a live operator. Implemented a custom NGram sentence tokenizer to build a business logic upon that.
To construct this microservice, I integrated four distinct internal services, utilizing a suite of technologies:

Leveraged Asterisk 18 (AGI and the asterisk-java library), Kotlin, and Spring Boot (including Core, Actuator, and Web modules), along with micrometer for monitoring, and slf4j coupled with log4j2. Additionally, I incorporated the Silero models for accurate voice transcribing and synthesis.
Ensured robust service functionality through rigorous testing with JUnit, Mockito and Wiremock.

Conducted set of  production A / B experiments with detailed reports about it’s results including actionable follow-ups.
Underwriting


company Revolut

The stack mostly contained micro-libraries. The mapping to well-known stack is following:

Broker - Alpha framework (written in-house)
Transactions - Alpha framework
ORM - JOOQ
DI - Guice
REST - Spark
regular stack: Java, Kotlin, Postgress, SQL, Bitbucket, Docker, Grafana, Google Cloud Platform, Teamcity.

5 microservices

Implemented features to automate a Credit Department Underwriting flow.
Fully maintained and developed features for a microservice. Implemented features of manual stage of the fully automated journey of credit department. Written unit and functional tests. Exposed some automatic jouney features for backoffice agents. Implemented a custom country-based permission system for backoffice.
Interacted with FE and BE engineers to establish an API contract and crucial implementation details.
Watchman activity: checked production errors, introduced fixes or follow-up tasks. Customized Newrelic to have relevant alerts.
Learned Kotlin and bunch of in-house business-specific frameworks and platform for CQRS, transactionability from scratch. Learned DDD principles to build a domain logic.

Revolut is a London-headquartered financial technology company that offers banking services allowing customers to transfer money to over 50 countries using more than 30 currencies. The Credit Underwriting platform assesses credit applications from all over the world to decide whether Revolut is ready to issue a loan.
REHA.Complete

Company: Deutsche Telekom IT Solutions
Project participation period: 2016-11-15 — 2020-12-31

The web CRM for Austrian government rehabilitation healthcare organization. The application handles the entire lifecycle of clinical orders, vital signs management, printing reports, diagnoses management, and other aspects of clinic operations. The system has the rich customizing of UI components. In addition, there was a lot of UI business automation implementation by feedback from doctors in the clinic.
Jasper Reports (Embedded + Standalone), PDFBox, Spring (IoC; REST), JPA: Hibernate, SQL, Oracle Database, Liquibase, Apache Tomcat, Servlets, Apache Camel, JUnit + Mockito, Maven, JProfiler, MapStruct, DozerMapper, Jackson Mapper, GitLab, Jenkins, JDBC.
Java 8: since 2017-11-21;
Java 6: since 2016-11-15;
The most challenging thing is application complexity. 800 database tables within the monolith, large team, 8-year legacy. Managed by constantly maintaining documentation and interacting with the team.

Implemented backend and frontend document PDF printing functionality.
115 features implemented. 492 bugs fixed.
Participated in the team of 14 developers, three automation test engineers, 1 UX engineer, 6 manual test engineers. The particular scrum team I worked in is of 5 people: test engineer, architect, team lead, 2 developers (one of them is me).
Processed code review requests on the daily basis.
Wrote technical patch notes describing my implementation.
Provided unit-test coverage for the implemented functionality.
Participated in team discussions related to designing the technical aspect of the solutions.
In the scope of migrating to test automation framework, conducted the 30-hour java introduction for automation test engineers: objects, interfaces, and other essential java topics.
Estimated efforts for upcoming CRs.
Mentored students through the Deutsche Telekom IT Solutions Java school ‘18 and ‘19.

telekom-healthcare.com/en/clinic-it/clinical-information-systems/rehabilitation-software
Techportal

Company: RT Labs

Techportal. The project’s deliverable was a module for the deployment tool used by the DevOps team. It provides web-interface job creation for installing Linux packages to multiple Linux hosts with several clicks.
Java 8, Spring (Core), Hibernate, git, React.

Implemented the core database design of 6 tables for the module.
Implemented the business layer features.
Implemented user web-UI module using React.

ETL module

Company: RT Labs

ETL for Monitoring System. Implemented the ETL module from scratch. Communicated with the expert to clarify the requirements.
Java 8, Spring (Core, Boot, REST), Linux.
Malware reporter bot

Company: VK
2014-02 — Sep 2015

C# SkypeBot automatically reports phishing and malware websites to hosters. One-man project to automate cutomser support routine. The Skype bot which reports phishing and malware websites to security organizations (like Phishtank) and to the web hosters. It helps to quickly report phishing websites found by the support first line engineers. Supports administrative commands, statistics.

Implemented application solo from scratch.
Acted as the subject field expert and as the main and the only developer.

C#, SQLite, SKYPE4COM.dll, git.

github.com/ivan-kopylove/quova-api
github.com/ivan-kopylove/mini-geo-tool

MGT: IP location tool

Company: VK
Jan 2013 — Jul 2015

Player support routine automation. Windows WPF IP location tool based on geo API. Quova geo API. The application for the Mail.Ru Games Support Investigation department. Application shows multiple IP’s geo information. Provides the quick batch processing (up to 2000 and more IP cached addresses per second) and visual grouping of the same IP networks. Hotkeys suppport. Custom database index. 2-level cache: in-memory, disk.

Acted as the subject field expert and as the main and the only developer.
Implemented database cache design to cache IP information from external API.

C#, SQLite, git, Quova Geo IP API

Kopylov Ivan - backend Java / Kotlin developer

15 days of sick leaves since 2016 (counted by payslips). More on the calm side of personality. Wellbeing-oriented person. Has been doing yoga since 2017.
Checklist- and note-taking powered developer.
Covers deliverables with unit- and integration tests.
Experience with application performance profiling.
Experience with databases: bottleneck identification, query optimization, schema design.
Experience with Maven, Gradle and continuous integration and delivery.
Fully embraces software engineering principles: separation of concerns, abstraction, modularity, incremental development, consistency, and applying design patterns.
Gives early notice of blocking issues and/or progress updates on sensitive issues.
Has an experience with CI / CD, DD (ubiquitous language, bounded contexts) and with the majority of extreme programming artifacts.
Experience in software life cycle (design, development, delivery, deployment, and support).
Has solid experience applying Java backend technologies (e.g.: Spring, Hibernate) within microservice environment.
Proactively clarifies obscure points. Hands-on approach to writing patch notes and resolution descriptions for deliverables.
Strong side is process-oriented mindset. Follows established processes. Gardner.
Suggests components’ improvements in a form of PR in atomic form so that it is reviewer can apply full focus to a particular points.
